KernelEntityBatchCreateRequest.description:
Batch request for creating (or resolving) kernel entities.

The request body wraps a list of single ``KernelEntityCreateRequest``
payloads.  The server processes them in order within a single
transaction so that ontology loaders (MONDO, HPO, UBERON, etc.) can
avoid the per-entity HTTP + commit overhead that dominates load times.

entities.description: Entities to create or resolve, processed in order. Capped at 500 per request to keep transactions bounded.
entities.title: Entities

KernelEntityBatchCreateRequest.title: KernelEntityBatchCreateRequest

KernelEntityBatchCreateResponse.description:
Response for batch create-or-resolve operations.

Returns one ``KernelEntityUpsertResponse`` per input entity in the same
order as the request, plus aggregate counts so callers can quickly tell
how much work happened.
KernelEntityBatchCreateResponse.title: KernelEntityBatchCreateResponse

KernelEntityCreateRequest.description: Request model for creating (or resolving) a kernel entity.

post.description:
Create or resolve up to 500 entities in a single transaction.

Designed for ontology loaders (MONDO has ~26k terms; HPO/UBERON/GO/CL
each have several thousand) where the per-entity HTTP + commit
overhead of the single-entity endpoint dominates load times.
Processing is sequential within the batch so identifier and exact-match
resolution semantics stay aligned with the single-entity path. Newly
created rows skip conflicting aliases instead of aborting the whole
chunk; validation failures still roll the batch back atomically.
post.summary: Create or resolve a batch of entities

schema.description: Direct relation types to scan.  Defaults to ASSOCIATED_WITH if not specified.
schema.title: Relation Type

parameters.description: Optional narrowing of candidate relations by source entity type.
parameters.name: source_entity_type

schema.description: Optional narrowing of candidate relations by source entity type.
schema.title: Source Entity Type

parameters.description: Optional narrowing of candidate relations by target entity type.
parameters.name: target_entity_type

schema.description: Optional narrowing of candidate relations by target entity type.
schema.title: Target Entity Type

parameters.description: Entity types that count as 'mechanism' bridges.  Defaults to BIOLOGICAL_PROCESS, SIGNALING_PATHWAY, MOLECULAR_FUNCTION, PROTEIN_DOMAIN.
parameters.name: intermediate_entity_type

schema.description: Entity types that count as 'mechanism' bridges.  Defaults to BIOLOGICAL_PROCESS, SIGNALING_PATHWAY, MOLECULAR_FUNCTION, PROTEIN_DOMAIN.
schema.title: Intermediate Entity Type

parameters.description: Maximum bridge path length (in edges).  Default 2 preserves the legacy 2-hop bridge test.  Must be in [2, 4]: length 1 is a direct relation (different question), and depths beyond 4 in a biomedical graph rarely reflect meaningful mechanism chains.
parameters.name: max_hops

schema.description: Maximum bridge path length (in edges).  Default 2 preserves the legacy 2-hop bridge test.  Must be in [2, 4]: length 1 is a direct relation (different question), and depths beyond 4 in a biomedical graph rarely reflect meaningful mechanism chains.
schema.title: Max Hops

get.summary: Find direct relations that lack an N-hop bridge (default 2) through mechanism entities

parameters.description: Required starting point for the multi-hop traversal.
parameters.name: seed_entity_id

schema.description: Required starting point for the multi-hop traversal.
schema.title: Seed Entity Id

parameters.description: Maximum BFS depth.  Length 1 has no gaps by definition; values greater than 5 are rejected to bound traversal cost.
parameters.name: max_path_length

schema.description: Maximum BFS depth.  Length 1 has no gaps by definition; values greater than 5 are rejected to bound traversal cost.
schema.title: Max Path Length

parameters.description: Optional list of relation types to restrict the traversal.
parameters.name: relation_type

schema.description: Optional list of relation types to restrict the traversal.
schema.title: Relation Type

get.summary: Find entities reachable from a seed via multi-hop paths but with no direct edge
